page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-22 is a compute-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It features 22 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 11 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 44.0 GB of system memory, yielding a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for deep learning and matrix operations. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates steady processing capabilities, achieving a PassMark CPU score of 28,645.69 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,194.0. Single-core performance is marked by a stress-ng score of 1,623.99 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1,730.0.

Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1,498.59 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 44.52 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server achieves 23.96 million operations per second in Redis SET tests and a static web serving throughput of 30.84 GB/sec. While it lacks local NVMe storage, its high compute-to-memory ratio and advanced instruction sets make it appropriate for CPU-bound tasks such as software compilation, web serving, and moderate-scale machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-22 is a compute-optimized instance featuring 22 dedicated vCPUs and 44.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor running at 2.7 GHz. Utilizing the x86_64 architecture, it includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX, which accelerate vector and matrix computations. The instance does not bundle local storage or GPU hardware. In performance testing, it delivers a PassMark CPU score of 28,645.69, a Geekbench multi-core score of 12,194.0, and a Redis SET throughput of 23.96 million operations per second. With a resource profile of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU, this server provides a high compute-to-memory density for CPU-intensive workloads. It is appropriate for tasks such as software compilation, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highcpu-22 is a compute-optimized server with 22 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 44 GB RAM, and advanced instruction sets, delivering balanced performance for CPU-intensive workloads.

tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and advanced matrix extensions for high-throughput CPU-bound workloads.

Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix math
23.96 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Optimized for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based inference
